summary: New table editor: Cursor jumps to first position after entering one or two characters

description.text: ### Description **Expected behaviour:** When entering text into a table cell, the cursor should remain positioned after the last entered character. **Actual behaviour:** After typing one or two characters into a table cell, the cursor jumps to the beginning of the cell content. This results in mixed-up text, such as ›oN‹ instead of No or ›sYe‹ instead of Yes. ### Reproducing 1. Add a table to a Zettlr document. 2. Type text into any cell. 3. Observe whether the cursor jumps to the first position after one or two characters. ### Zettlr version 3.6.0-nightly ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) summary: GUI: Editor unexpectedly shifts when entering text in the new table editor

description.text: ### Description **Expected behaviour:** When entering text into table cells using the new table editor, the visible portion of the document should remain fixed — the editor should not scroll or shift unexpectedly. **Actual behaviour:** After clicking into a table cell, the visible portion of the document sometimes shifts — slightly or significantly — both immediately after clicking and again after typing text into the cell. Position before entering text: Position after entering text: ### Reproducing 1. Add a table to a Zettlr document that is longer than the visible editor area. 2. Click into several table cells and enter text. 3. Observe whether the visible portion of the document jumps after clicking and/or typing. ### Zettlr version 3.6.0-nightly ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method summary: Inconsistent behaviour when pasting into cells in the new table editor

description.text: ### Description **Expected behaviour:** Pasting a string into a table cell should reliably save it. **Actual behaviour:** 1. I inserted a new table using the row/column selector menu. 2. I pasted four strings into the first column of the first four rows — all were saved correctly. 3. When pasting a string into the first column of the fifth row, the content disappeared after about one or two seconds. 4. Repeating the paste caused the content to vanish instantly two or three times. 5. The only reliable workaround was pressing Tab immediately after pasting, which apparently forced the editor to save the cell content. ### Reproducing 1. Insert a table using the row/column selector menu. 2. Paste strings into cells of the first column, one per row. 3. Continue until the pasted content disappears instead of being saved. ### Zettlr version 3.6.0-nightly ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) _No response_ summary: "Insert Table" entry of the right-click context menu is without function

description.text: ### Description **Expected behaviour:** When using the "Insert Table" entry found in the right-click context menu, insert a table at the current position in the document. **Actual behaviour:** When choosing the "Insert Table" menu entry, Zettlr does nothing. ### Reproducing * Open the right-click context menu inside a Zettlr file * chose "Insert Table" ### Zettlr version 3.6.0 summary: feat: Allow global search to be cancelled

description.text:
## Description
This PR adds the ability to cancel/re-trigger an already in-flight global search run.

This feature was present in the past, but got removed at some point. While I'm not sure why the removal happened, I thought of reintroducing the feature, as it might be useful in certain cases where a user wants to cancel a long running search.

## Changes
- Added a new "Cancel" button next to the "Search" button
  - It is only enabled if a search is running
  - If clicked, it will result in the current search run being cancelled
- The search button is now also enabled during an already running search
  - If clicked, it will trigger a new search run based on the current value of the search field

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: Ubuntu WSL, Windows 11

summary: File Manager Display Bugs whilst changing Display Mode with Search Pane selected.

description.text: ### Description When the Search Pane is selected instead of the File Manager in the main window, toggling the File Manager's **Display Mode in Preferences** will cause the File Manager's appearance to be set to an incorrect visual state. ### Reproducing This can be consistently reproduced by doing the following. 1. Switch the File Manager to the Search Pane. 2. Go to `Preferences > File Manager`, and then switch the **Display Mode** to **Combined**, then to **Expanded**, then back to **Combined**. 3. Go back to the main Zettlr window, and switch the Search Pane to the File Manager. ### Zettlr version Zettlr 3.6.0 Build f3d8800 ### Installation Method Flathub ### Installation Method (Other) _No response_ ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Architecture - [x] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version Linux Mint Debian Edition ### Additional Information This was also tested on the **Zettlr 3.6.0-nightly Build a7c1978**, as an App-Image with the same results. Results appear to be consistent. This bug is temporarily and fixes on a reload of Zettlr using `F5` with no further side effects.

summary: Setting Align Column Center breaks tables.

description.text: ### Description In the Nightly build, it is possible to cause table behaviour to completely break by setting Align Column Center. This causes both **rendering** and **behaviour** breakages, as seen in the below image. ### Reproducing 1. Create a table 2. Turn on Align Column Center in one of the top rows. ### Zettlr version Zettlr 3.6.0-nightly (Build a7c1978) ### Installation Method From the Website or GitHub ### Installation Method (Other) App Image ### Your Platform - [ ] Windows - [ ] macOS - [x] Linux ### Architecture - [x] Intel 64bit - [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon) ### Operating System Version Linux Mint Debian Edition ### Additional Information _No response_
